signals.reasoning: The live SOL short has now fallen to about 42% peak PnL retention and is flagged urgent, while the active veto headline still leans relief-style against the short. This is no longer a clean thesis hold; it is a protect-the-remainder cover.
BTC is holding up on the relief headline, which makes fresh downside bets less attractive under veto. Better to stay neutral once the live SOL short is cleaned up.
ETH is also leaning into the broader recovery, so it is not a compelling bearish follow-on while the event brake is active.
TST remains far too extended and volatile to trust as a fresh trade. Even with squeeze fuel, it is not where disciplined management should go next.
WCT is extremely extended and still event-sensitive. There is no clean entry edge here under a live veto.
STABLE still has squeeze characteristics, but the relief-style event headline and the already-advanced move make it a poor fresh decision right now.
MERL is just a rebound with no cleaner edge than the rest of the board. Watch-only.
VVV still reads more like recovery and short-covering than a fresh trend continuation. It is not clean enough to buy or short.
BRETT is constructive, but the move is already well underway and the live veto argues against forcing a new trade.
ZEREBRO remains noisy and high beta, which makes it a poor candidate in a headline-sensitive regime.
CHILLGUY is still a recovery name, not a clean new signal. There is no reason to force it here.
MON is not giving a strong enough edge in either direction to justify action under veto. Neutral is cleaner.
VIRTUAL is constructive but not distinct enough from the rest of the rebound board to justify a fresh trade.
ZEC still has some bearish character, but it is not clean enough to short into a relief-style event headline.
0G has squeeze fuel, but it is still too unstable and too event-sensitive to trust here.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already mature enough that it is not a clean fresh short anymore.
BOME is still too thin and noisy to trust during a live geopolitical event cycle.
JTO still has squeeze fuel, but the event veto and mixed recovery structure keep it from being a clean actual long.
BLUR has some rebound potential, but it is still not clean enough to justify a new trade under the active event brake.
WLD remains structurally constructive, but it does not offer a better immediate decision than cleaning up the open SOL short.
